title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return for the Celtics' game against the Washington Wizards after missing Thursday for injury management.
Tatum is playing under a minutes restriction due to a right Achilles injury he sustained 10 months ago.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic remains out for the Wizards game.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' starring Alan Ritchson streams on Netflix, featuring a soldier dealing with PTSD during Army Ranger training.
'Zootopia 2' with Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin is available on Disney+, following the duo's police adventures.
HBO Max's 'Rooster' with Steve Carell focuses on campus comedy and family dynamics, created by Bill Lawrence.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Alijah Vera-Tucker joins the Patriots to strengthen the offensive line after Drake Maye was sacked 68 times.
Vera-Tucker has started at every offensive line position except center; primarily played right guard and left guard.
Vera-Tucker was convinced to join the Patriots by former teammate Morgan Moses and values their winning culture.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora names Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as team leaders for the 2026 season.
Trevor Story showcased leadership in 2025 with a healthy season, leading the Red Sox in key offensive stats.
Garrett Crochet excelled as a starter in 2025, leading MLB in strikeouts and being compared to elite pitchers.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC debuts in NWSL against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium, marking its first regular season game.
The club's new coach, Filipa Patão, plans a possession-style game, with a potential 4-4-2 formation.
Gotham FC, featuring stars like Rose Lavelle, poses a challenging opponent for Boston's inaugural match.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are debating a bill to end the biannual clock change, backed by Senate President Karen E. Spilka.
Proponents claim permanent time can reduce sleep disruptions, health issues, and enhance productivity, citing various health studies.
The proposal to move to permanent Atlantic Standard Time is under Senate review, with effects on daylight perception considered.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announced the 2026 parade and festival for Saturday, June 6, starting in Copley Square.
The celebration includes a parade, noon festival at Boston Common, and a block party from 2 to 8 p.m.
Mayor Michelle Wu praised the event's inclusivity and the organization's efforts in fostering joy and community.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ordered the release of a 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough, Massachusetts.
ICE detained the girl in a Marlborough parking lot, claiming she was with gang members linked to a home invasion.
The girl, held in New York, was returned to Boston and reunited with her family, awaiting an immigration court notice.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins face scoring struggles post-Olympic break, despite initial high ranking in NHL goals per game.
David Pastrnak's recent performance shows limited 5-on-5 success; team relies heavily on Zacha line for scoring.
Coach Marco Sturm contemplates forward line reshuffle to spark scoring, potentially integrating prospect James Hagens.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne from Geneva, Switzerland, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise $10,000 for the Stepping Strong Center.
Cogne, a visiting researcher at Harvard Medical School, focuses on neurorehabilitation to help trauma survivors regain independence.
The fundraising efforts aim to support trauma research and rehabilitation at Brigham and Women's Hospital in Boston.
